---
title: "teachMe"
date: "2026-03-10"
categories: [Next.js, OpenAI, Supabase, Vercel, Claude Code, TypeScript]
---

## Overview

teachMe is an AI reading companion designed for ADHD learners. Users paste or upload any dense text — a textbook excerpt, article, or document — and receive a streaming AI-generated explanation read aloud with natural text-to-speech. While listening, they can pause, click any paragraph, ask a follow-up question, and hear the answer read back in context before playback resumes where it left off.

The core insight: passive reading fails ADHD brains. teachMe converts it into an active, audio-first experience anchored to the source document — not a generic chatbot.

**Target user:** College students and adults with ADHD who re-read the same paragraph multiple times without retaining it, and learn better when they can ask questions in the moment.

> "Paste anything. Understand it. Remember it."

## Live Product Demo

<div class="d-flex justify-content-center gap-3 my-4">
  <a class="btn btn-primary d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://teach-me-nu.vercel.app"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open live app">
    <i class="fa-solid fa-window-restore me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    Open App in New Tab
  </a>

  <a class="btn btn-outline-primary d-inline-flex align-items-center"
     href="https://github.com/theronap/teachMe"
     target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     aria-label="Open GitHub source">
    <i class="fa-brands fa-github me-2" aria-hidden="true"></i>
    View Source on GitHub
  </a>
</div>

<iframe src="https://teach-me-nu.vercel.app/read"
        style="width:100%; height:900px; border:none;"
        allowfullscreen>
</iframe>

## Technical Details

**Framework/Stack:** Next.js 16 (App Router, React 19), TypeScript

**AI:** Vercel AI SDK — Claude Haiku (explanation/simplification), OpenAI tts-1 (TTS), Whisper (voice input)

**Backend:** Supabase (auth + PostgreSQL with Row-Level Security)

**Deployment:** Vercel

**Tools Used:**

- Claude Code
- Supabase (auth, database, RLS)
- OpenAI (TTS + transcription)
- Anthropic / Claude Haiku (LLM)
- Vercel (hosting + edge functions)

## Product Requirements Document

**Product:** teachMe — AI Reading Companion for ADHD Learners

**Objective:** Build an audio-first reading tool that turns any pasted or uploaded text into an AI-narrated, interactive study session — saving sessions to a personal library.

**Requirements:**

1. **Core Reading Flow**
   - User submits text via paste, PDF upload, or URL fetch
   - App streams an AI-generated explanation (Claude Haiku) in real time
   - Text-to-speech reads the explanation aloud, paragraph by paragraph
   - Source text and explanation displayed side-by-side; source highlights as audio plays

2. **Inline Q&A**
   - Hovering any explanation paragraph reveals an "Ask" prompt
   - Submitting a question during TTS pauses audio, reads the question aloud, streams the answer, reads it back, then offers to resume
   - Follow-up questions supported within the same annotation card

3. **Reading Modes**
   - **Explain** — plain-English breakdown of the source text
   - **Simplify** — shorter, simpler version of the same content
   - **Study Guide** — structured outline with key points

4. **TTS Controls**
   - Speed: 0.75×–2×; default 1.25×
   - Voice presets: Nova, Shimmer, Alloy, Echo
   - Multi-slot prefetch buffer (3 chunks ahead) for gapless playback
   - Jump to any paragraph via "Play here" button

5. **Auth + Persistence**
   - Email magic link authentication via Supabase Auth
   - Documents auto-save to Supabase on generation and on each annotation
   - Row-Level Security: users can only read/write their own documents
   - Library page: list, reopen, and delete saved sessions

6. **Input Modes**
   - Paste (plain text)
   - File upload (PDF, TXT)
   - URL fetch
   - Voice input (Whisper transcription)

## User Testing Findings (Sprint 3)

Conducted 3 moderated sessions (~10 min each) with college students matching the target ICP.

**Key findings:**

- The two most differentiating features (TTS and inline Q&A) were not discovered by 2 of 3 users — discoverability is the top UX problem
- Quiz/testing mode was the most-requested missing feature; one user invented a workaround by typing questions manually
- Side-by-side source view was consistently cited as the clearest differentiator from ChatGPT
- Input friction (URL not working, PDF formatting lost on paste) blocked the session start for 2 users

**Top priority changes identified:**

1. Surface TTS and Q&A on first load via onboarding hint
2. Add auto-generated quiz mode from the explanation
3. Fix URL and PDF input reliability; preserve paste formatting
4. Sharpen landing page copy around the differentiators
